_disclaimer: PROVISIONAL engineering defaults — NOT vet-validated. Pending baseline research (docs/15). Tune only with veterinary sign-off; log any change as an ADR (docs/02).

global_baselines._comment: docs/08 Global Default Baselines (medium adult dog, resting). dog_baselines rows override heart_rate/respiratory/temperature per-dog; there is no per-dog motion baseline column, so motion_activity always uses this default.

scoring_rules._comment: docs/08 Scoring Rules (rule-v1). hr_ratio = heart_rate_bpm / baseline heart_rate_bpm; rr_ratio = respiratory_rate_bpm / baseline respiratory_rate_bpm. Tiers are evaluated as [min, max) unless max is null (unbounded). Rules are skipped entirely when their input field is null (docs/07: 'the classifier skips rules for null fields').

posture_moving_with_high_motion._comment: docs/08: 'Posture = moving sustained with high motion' +1. Doc does not define 'high motion' numerically for this rule; reuses the motion_restlessness tier-1 floor (0.6) as the threshold. Flagged as an inference — revisit once trend/posture history is available.

environmental_amplifier._comment: docs/08: ambient > 32C OR humidity > 80% (heat stress context) +1.

rising_trend._comment: docs/08: 'stress score rose across last 3 readings' +1. Interpreted as: the 3 most recent prior stress_classifications scores for this dog, ordered oldest-to-newest, are strictly increasing. Requires at least 3 prior scores; skipped otherwise.

context_rules._comment: Context-only reason codes: they NEVER change the stress score (docs/08 scores heat but not cold), but they feed the owner-facing 'why' and Care Insights combinations (e.g. cold + stressed -> warm bed tip). Thresholds are provisional and vet-tunable like everything else here.

device_alerts._comment: Device-health alert thresholds (docs/11 'Battery is low'). Provisional; ops/vet tunable.

level_thresholds._comment: docs/08 Score -> level mapping. Evaluated as [min, max] inclusive; high has no upper bound.

validation_ranges._comment: docs/07 Validation Ranges. Values outside range are flagged is_valid=false at the row level; the out-of-range field is stored as null in the structured telemetry_readings column (raw_payload always keeps the true received value verbatim per ADR-003). These are sensor/hardware plausibility bounds, not clinical thresholds.
